sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the images, the purple square hasn't moved from its position. The batch of 7 clicks didn't seem to move it further. Let me check the current state more carefully. The purple square center was reported as (5, 31) which seems wrong - that's near the top. Let me re-examine.
Wait, there are TWO purple objects detected - one at (5, 31) and one at (51, 12). The one at (5, 31) is probably part of the "STEP" text at the top. The actual purple square is at (51, 12).
But looking at the images, the purple square appears to be in roughly the same position as before. The clicks I made were at positions (29,34), (27,36), etc. - further up the path. But the square didn't move.
This suggests that clicking further ahead on the path doesn't move the square - it only moves one step at a time. Or perhaps the square needs to be clicked on directly to move it.
Let me re-examine: the first click at (53, 10) moved it from (59, 4) to (53, 10). Then clicking at (51, 12) moved it to (51, 12). So clicking on the NEXT path position moves the square there.
But then I batched 7 clicks at positions (29,34) through (17,46), skipping many intermediate positions. The square is still at (51, 12).
So the rule seems to be: clicking on a path position moves the square there ONLY if it's the next position in sequence. Since I skipped from (51,12) to (29,34), the clicks were ignored.
I need to click each intermediate position: (49, 14), (47, 16), (45, 18), (43, 20), (41, 22), (39, 24), (37, 26), (35, 28), (33, 30), (31, 32), then (29, 34), etc.
Let me batch all remaining positions in order.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The square is still at (51, 12). I need to click each intermediate path position in order.
# Path: (57,6), (55,8), (53,10), (51,12), (49,14), (47,16), (45,18), (43,20), (41,22), (39,24), (37,26), (35,28), (33,30), (31,32), (29,34), (27,36), (25,38), (23,40), (21,42), (19,44), (17,46)
# Current: index 3 (51,12)
# Need to click indices 4-20 in order

path = [(57, 6), (55, 8), (53, 10), (51, 12), (49, 14), (47, 16), (45, 18), (43, 20), (41, 22), (39, 24), (37, 26), (35, 28), (33, 30), (31, 32), (29, 34), (27, 36), (25, 38), (23, 40), (21, 42), (19, 44), (17, 46)]

# Remaining positions from index 4 onward
remaining = path[4:]
print(f"Need to click {len(remaining)} positions: {remaining}")

actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c} for r, c in remaining]
action(actions)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
